text:
We can’t say enough good things about T-Mobile. We had switched to ATT in July 2024. We were promised by doing so that we would be receiving two $200 Visa cards. After a runaround for several months we gave up and got screwed by ATT!
We signed up with T-Mobile two weeks ago under their promotion. They are paying us $800 and $622 for the phones we bought from ATT and were still paying for. That money has now been deposited into our account exactly as promised and even less time than we were told. 
Coverage is every bit as good as the other big two carriers!

Recently I was the victim of T-mobile Cramming.  Adding services to any encounter without the customers knowledge or consent, in order to generate revenue and sales commissions.  (Apparently it’s not uncommon occurrence at the T-Mobile stores, especially non-corporate stores)  Phone protection and extra data plans.  E.g: search reddit for: is adding insurance on phones without asking  I am a senior, which seems to be a target group.  They apparently get a commission for adding services without your permission, and the T-Mobile customer services rep told me it is "company policy" not to give full refunds for services.  (Even for services you never ordered.)

🔒 T-Mobile allowed unauthorized transfer of phone number – serious incident in the Czech Republic

I’d like to share an unfortunate experience that happened to my grandfather, a long-term customer of T-Mobile Czech Republic.

Due to an internal error by a T-Mobile employee, his phone number was transferred to a different person without his knowledge or consent. As a result, he lost access to his number for four full days.

This posed serious risks – including the inability to contact family in case of emergency and a potential breach of personal security, as the number may have been linked to essential services like banking or two-factor authentication.

We filed an official complaint. While T-Mobile admitted the mistake, the offered compensation did not reflect the seriousness of the incident. We had expected a more proactive and responsible approach to customer data protection and service integrity.

📢 I’m not sharing this out of emotion, but as a warning to other customers: if something similar happens to you, don’t stay silent – demand accountability and fair resolution.

I sincerely hope that T-Mobile, both in the Czech Republic and globally, improves their internal processes to prevent such incidents from happening again.

Had a horrible experience trying to get a billing question answered. They insisted I use their stupid app and could not recall my pin they were unable to reset it. Spent over 1 hour and the matter is still unresolved it also appears that they fraudlently changed the terms of my contract.  Avoid T-mobile the terms of their plans mean nothing. After I left a pungent review on their followup T-Mobil contacted me and proceeded to try and sell me an expenisve package for overseas calling and they did not reverse the 25 cents a minute call which is ridiculiously high fee. I told the agent that I would terminate our 10 year relationship with the company.

I signed up for a phone plan and got a phone for free, but I never activated that phone with a the line because I brought in my own phone already, so I just had been using the free phone with wifi. Well it is paid off now after being over 2 years of being a loyal customer and called them to unlock it and they said it cannot be unlocked bc it has to be connected to a phone line for 40 days. well BS. stupid policy and no manager can override. So I am now stuck with this free phone with Tmobile locked. Many other communication companies out there to switch too, and they have much better customer service. Stupid company, stupid policy.

I ve been a loyal customer with tmobile for 2yrs.  No late 
payments, no 
problems.I even encouraged others to switch to tmobile. On Feb 28, I made my payment on time, as always. I saw it deducted from my balance on my bank app.  Shortly afterwards,I received a receipt and a thank you. Over 2weeks later, I was notified I didn't pay. I forwarded my receipt. Service was restored with a notice of payment plan. WHAT! After reading notification, I had to pay close to $400 for late fees, requesting a live rep, and restored fees for 2 lines @ $20 each. I wasn't given time to  contact my bank to verify payment wasnt sent or what the problem was.I tried to dial 611 but response was always "service currently unavailable". I tried to access my tmobile app. It required a 6 digit ID code sent to my text. If I received it at all, it was 45 mins later and had expired. Speaking with a live rep cost $10 each time.As a loyal customer, I was confident it would be easily resolved.  I was patient and respectful and if I did reach a live rep, we were often disconnected, which required $10  more to call back. Im sure this was not a mistake but rather by design.I have had no service for a week. I have begged for a reasonable solution.  After finally learning from my bank, it was deducted from my acct instead of my debit card. Bank deductions take up to 3 days and debit cards are immediate.  After fraudulent activity in February, my card and bank acct was assigned new numbers . So tmobile not receiving funds was possible but I should have been notified by my bank and tmobile , much sooner than 2 1/2 weeks. It was the fault of nobody and everybody involved.  The bank offered to confirm the situation with tmobile but they were not interested. The fact I had been loyal and had a good pymnt history was not considered.All they would accept was immediate payment or no service.  It was clear the rep was sympathetic but had no power. My history, sending customers, being a 66 yr old widow with no land line, promise of paying with debit card in full on April 2nd, which would immediately be received, NOTHING WORKED! I asked for a email or phone contact for corporate office, but that is not allowed. THAT ALONE IS A RED FLAG! I have never dealt with a company so abrasive and unreasonable in my life. The poor customer service is not the reps, it's demands from corporate. I ve learned tmobile is owned by a German Telecommunications company , with corporate offices in Washington state and Charlotte , NC. They may as well be on Mars. I promised to pay asap if they actually did not receive funds from my bank. I wasn't trying to cheat from making my payment,  but it was impossible to get any mercy or understanding. If I was habitually late with payments, I could understand, but tmobile is losing a great customer and hopefully, I will convince others to leave. Basically, I was strong armed. It was " we don't care if u saw the pymnt deducted and have a receipt, our word is above all else and pay now or no service". I intend to report my claims to cyber crimes division of FBI, BBB and every regulatory agency that is possible.  Tmobile needs to be investigated immediately.  If not, I hope others will join me to complain and spread the customer treatment until they practice ethics and respectfulness. If not, hopefully losing enough customers will put them out of business.  I never have experienced such lack of customer appreciation, for a situation tmobile was responsible for partially. It's as though their mentality is we have plenty of customers and losing my business was a non factor. Please, if u are a customer, leave now. I didn't believe it would happen to me, but it did. If u are not with tmobile, do NOT be fooled by their false claims.  Please, read all the horrible reviews on tmobile. I was hoping for a reasonable solution, but I didn't even get an apology. I'm sure they are confident I will just give up and go away.  Thisvtime, I have the determination and resolve to make sure American customers are appreciated, not disposable!

Horrible 5G business internet. Slow speeds, high latency, incompetent tech support. 5G router has no configurable settings. Every tech support person wants to send you into the T life app, get you going thru set up then the app says its business line and you cannot use the app. Terrible customer service in overseas call centers with high latency where you can't hardly have a conversation with them. 

Stay far far away from this company as they peddle junk service and horrible customer service

Too many problems occurred when I had issue's with my phone dropping all my incoming and outgoing calls.
I spent almost a month of daily contact with customer service that was like a full-time job to maneuver through they're customer service that was no help just a lot of frustration. The outcome was to have the problem fixed by an outside source in a matter of minutes. T-Mobile couldn't admit that the 5G service in that area was the problem. I paid off my contract and closed my account and they locked up my phone. DO NOT DO BUSINESS WITH T-MOBILE!!

I was scammed at a convention into switching from my carrier by a FREE PHONE Offer. When I received my 1st bill, I saw it was not free at all.  I called TMobile and reached a person I could barely understand and was assured the PROMOTION would be applied. It has been  a year past and I am still paying for my FREE PHONE.  And, by the way, the 50% DEPOSIT I put down, I was told was going towards my accessories I bought actually went towards that FREE phone and NOT towards the accessories.  This has been a troublesome thing to me because when I sign on the iPad, I only sign a BLANK screen.  I was shown a screen which looked like everything was all in order all the numbers looked correct.  However, the screen that I signed was completely blank and now I wonder is that how this scam happened.  Or, Did the Sales Rep just Flat out Lie to Me and Tell it was Free and Fake me out with the blank screen?  Worst part is, I was a Vendor Right Across the way from T-Mobil....I WOULD NEVER DO THIS TO ANY CUSTOMER TO EARN MY COMMISSION.

I was told I would get a $200 Rebate/Voucher towards the accessories in my email and that never came either.  Otherwise I would have never had bought the ear-pods the Sales Rep talked me into.

When I called T-Mobile and tried to explain that I was at a Convention and that I was a Vendor as well and made the Purchase under the offer of a free phone, the rep said I signed the contract. Of course I did. And what Proof did I have that I was offered the FREE PHONE to switch?  

I have never written a review before in my life as  about a Sales Rep to beware of as I live off of Commissions myself. However, there seems to be many types of Scams out there.  Technology today HAS PROVIDED a new avenue of thief and companies.

I will keep paying for my FREE PHONE and when I am done, I WILL certainly switch to a different provider and hopefully actually this time actually get an updated model phone!!

Channel: RETAIL
StoreId: 489E
Store Address: 700 Convention Center Blvd, McAllen, TX 78501
Store Rep Name: Elie Barrera
Dealer Code: 3058193

I have been with T-Mobile for around 12 years and I had the Simple Choice plan. I checked every year or so, but no current plan was a better value. I kept this plan because I was under the impression they could not raise my rate. That had been implied during their "Un-carrier" campaign.

It turns out T-Mobile is susceptible to as much greed as the other carriers and they are no longer the "un-carrier." I was notified my plan was going up by $5 per line - even my "free" lines. I understand by law I need to pay tax on the free lines, but why were they charging $5 each for those lines?

I am shopping around for a new carrier. Even if I end up with the same price per month, I will leave T-Mobile on just principle.

I went into the T-Mobile store in Menomonee Falls Wi. with a panic as I had an issue on my old cell phone. The 2 salespeople took my phone and after working on it patiently for an hour returned what I deleted on my phone. I will be going there when I get my new phone.

The company could improve... but it won't.
For the last three months, every night between 9:00pm and 10:00pm there is no internet service.  By no service I mean zero, not slow.
If you call at that time you will get the offshore "customer service". You go through their start up  nonsense and they tell you to restart your gateway... DUH! Done that ! Thanks for nothing.

edits.text:
I’ve had T-Mobile internet for less than a month. It has been down three times with no notice, even when it’s planned maintenance.
This is 2023 T-Mobile, you should have;
1 sent out notices 
2 have an automated line for outages because your outsource help is less than worthless.
3 have number that will update on your outages.
Knock knock, it’s almost 2024.

I was offered the opportunity to upgrade my phone for ‘free’ yesterday by T-Mobile, sounds good.
I trade in my phone for a new phone.
I take out the SIM card, put it in the new phone... Works great. But I’m charged a $35 + tax connection fee... For nothing.
I did all the ‘work’!

text: T-mobile representatives in store and online LIE on multiple occasions about price or promise of service. I ended up being stranded on vacation with no service, despite of the fact that I purchased the service specifically for international travel. I spent hours on the phone resolving connectivity. Last conversation was with the agent Odeza, who assured me that she will call me back on March18th and will resolve the last issue to "restore my trust with the company". However, not only did she not call, but when i called I was told no notes were. CS reps i reached were SUPER rude!!! their rep YELLED at me!!! interrupted me! Accused me of getting adjustments to my bill due to them over charging me!

edits.text:
1. Took 2 weeks, 5 calls and 2 in person visits to open an account 
2. Price quoted was not true. Hidden fees! That I was assured I wouldn’t have 
3. A months later turned out I’m being charged for 2 accounts instead of one - more hours on the phone. Took 5 different CS agents to actually get a knowledgeable one to resolve it and get back to one number 
4. Still overcharged - more calls to iron it out 
5. Being charged monthly with no delays or mistakes YET: 
6. Every time I travel, I end up stranded with no connectivity. Not even 3G, though I’m paying for 5G
7. Trying to resolve it on a trip - you need to login, to login, you need to update our app! Once it’s updated.. guess what?! I inconvenienced them by complaining of having no service at their “off hours” and I need to call back later! I’m paying to get service 24/7, 365 days a year!!! 
Yet… the time I need it, it’s simply not there!

I called to open and account and port my existing number (from family plan) to this new account. 15 days later, 6 representatives on the phone and 2 visits to the physical store: 
1. The price per month is higher than was quoted on opening the account and the plan is different
2. the porting was problematic and i had to deal with it with 5 different people
3. once the "temporary" number was canceled, i was locked out of my account as this was the number still used for text to log me in
4. I was charged for 2 lines, when i only opened an account for one line and i explained this to 5 different people. 
5. incompetent Customer Service reps and multiple departments handing the same issue. 

End result: 15 days later i'm on a trip still dealing with it for hours and it's not done. 

Can't get "transfer my phone to a new T-mobile account" TODO off my list in 15 days.

text: This is the second scam Tmoble has pulled on me costing me hundreds between the two. A couple of years ago I switched but the phone wasn't available so I didn't switch. I checked & my money was returned. The next time I balanced my account I discovered hundreds were drafted for their internet, I never opened the box. I had at&t fiber the whole time. I jumped through all their hoops @ the store on line and phone they just gave me a big run around!!! This second * im with metro by tmoble, I bought a S25 ultra and after 4 out of biz I finally found a store to switch the Sim card to my new phone. I paid my bill while I was there and it cleared my bank I wasn't given credit so after 5 days of trying I had no choice but pay twice. Before I left I was given a new tablet, no cost no nothing! Great right? WRONG! My phone bill doubled so again I got the run around. Now I've paid twice for the same month and they doubled my bill for a FREE TABLET! I signed nothing and agreed to nothing, they said here its free & then the extortion! To keep my number i had no option but pay! So that three x's no credit for payment, charged for "free" router and unused service that I didn't want or use! And a FREE TABLET that I didn't ask for and was told like with the router there was no cost.

T Mobail is very costly company.Their sell are many product but some goods are damaged in packaged.Customer service are not good and they can not delivery in time.So that is not good company for many people....

The worst service.  When I got on with a phone and internet plan I got quoted $85/mo.  After that every month I had used up my GB so I had to pay more to get my internet back on.  So from $85 my plan went to $150.  The worst company.  Don’t use them.  They’ll give you a cheap plan in the beginning and eventually it will be twice as much

Charged for whats app calls explaining to us it was because our internet service dropped but we have wireless home Tmobile router service so makes no sense.  Predatory event.  Service had no explanation.  Cash grab.

Our son needed better service in washington state so our family switched to t mobile on the 4th this month.
We have 4 phones in 3 different states.So sim cards were mailed out to be activated.We were told in Mt Vernon there was a 30 day period to change phones out. on the 10 we went to mesa az store to change phones 2 workers and half dozen customer after 1 1/2 hr we left to come back today the 19th .we were told in mesa it only 14 day and its been 18. I called from store to mt vernon and had both manager talk.
mesa would only sell us a phone out right even though mt vernon agreed to do paper work to make our deal we were promised complete. we then got asked to leave and go to different location for asking question on policy . I have a recording of your manager I believe would helpful for customer service.Any way left store sat in car an mt Vernon store ordered it in minutes to be mailed out in 2 days. 
we were 7 years with old provider doubt will be with T 
Mobile that long , Mesa manager Paul at 1923 Signal Butte is truly arrogant, rude, and no patience for us  older  customers

I spoke with a representative Abigail and she bragged about being the best sales representative. We spent an hour on the telephone collecting my information, she let me to believe that the order had gone through and that I would receive the cellphone in two-three days  She didn't have the common courtesy to tell me the order did not go through. I knew something was wrong when I didn't receive an order number or confirmation email. The follow up call with Sheinamae was not any better with her "0h well ,what can you do?" attitude

Maybe, I can`t created the full opinion, since I used the service of this American operator, once when I`m used to take part in a cultural exchange program. Though, the quality of connection was decent, no disrupts in mobile internet connections that worked much faster than Wi-fi in my hometown;)) Price was unknown for me, probably they didn`t give the best plan, so I think it a good one.

I would  leave  a 0 but not possible  I wanted  to upgrade  my internet  and was lied to by employee  I  have slower  speed now and a shit load of buffering I've  been a customer  for along time I don't  want something  for nothing  just what I paid for to me trust is everything  and you screwed me over

Charged for 2 lines since August 2023 for Single Line
Hi folks - this is March 2025 and we were on auto pay all the time. We cancelled a line in August 2023, and even then we have been charged for 2 lines for $100 for seniors. 

We bought it to the attention of the T mobile staff, they seem all in a different country, not sure if this will be resolved.

Can someone reach out to me to resolve this issue from T-Mobile. I need the refund for the 20 months of overcharging.

Their representative offered me a rate plan. 3 representatives later manipulated me and lied to me about the offer. The last representative cannot understand English and he is answering the phone to service English speaking people. T-Mobile lies to and manipulates their customers and will not be trusted with their "personal guarantee"!
The worst customer service in the store, and in the phone,,!  Customer service on the phone horrible, Nasty, vile people,! I wish there were zero stars!!

Tmobile has an agreement to never raise your rates. Well guess what they do not honor their own agreements. They are liars and have their reps tell you over and over again that we are breaking the law and our agreement to reward you as a customer. 

Tmobile has no sense of right or wrong and does not care if it violates the law as in the current world there appears to be no law unless you have billions of dollars and can buy what you want. I hope millions of people leave tmobile. They deserve it how they treat customers. I have been a customer for more than 10 years and they said they do not care.

We've been with T-Mobile for around 20 years and been rather satisfied with their cell service. The reason I gave only 3 stars is because their dining rewards program is a rip-off. They advertise 10% cash rewards if you dine at one of the listed restaurants on a Tuesday. I signed up for the program and ate at one of their listed restaurants. When I enquired as to why I didn't get the 10%, the response was that I didn't sign-up ahead of time which is untrue. They refused to correct the error. It's not a lot of money; however, it's the principle that bugs me! I haven't been using their rewards program since.

Tmobile employee lied talked us into buy a 2200.00 phone get a 1000 phone free! Well we ended up paying both phones! Tmobile was told 6 times and no one helped us instead qe had a 140.85 bill for years! Tmobile service drops and slows down  dont waste your money use starlink by Elon Musk or Verizon! We will be as soon as this contract is over!

I want to share accounts of my recent experiences with T-Mobile. Because I’ve spent so much needless time with the company, I’m cutting to the chase in these accounts.

I believe your company is engaged in fraud, and I will contact the Federal Trade Commission and other agencies over this matter.

I moved and, despite, living in an Ultra zone, service was abhorrently bad, ranging from as low as 5 or 10 mbps to about 100. Lots of 4K video was unwatchable or even unplayable on Apple TV+, which provides the best feeds. (Only after contacting CEO Mike Sievert's office did I learn that T-Mobile staff never should have transferred this service, as it was not offered in my new location.)

I called December 10, and all the agent was willing to do was provide a one-time $15 credit. I told her I was going to end the service because it was unusable and had to suggest to her that there must be a newer, better, faster modem. She sprang to life and put in my account notes that I should be given the G4AR modem.

Hours later, I dropped off my old modem and picked up the G4AR modem. A few days later, I discover in my account that I was being charged for a $40 second line, for the G4AR modem. This add-on was unauthorized and constitutes FRAUD.

I called December 13 and explained everything to an agent who seemed to understand my problem. My account’s recent activity lists that the old modem’s line was canceled, but on December 14, it was still listed. Is this a T-Mobile scam? Do you assure people action’s been taken but leave the charges on?

I called three times shortly thereafter. The first woman understood everything and eliminated the line for the old modem. She then told me that I’d be on the Home Internet Plus plan and that I would pay $40 monthly and would be grandfathered in. She told me that T-Mobile was running a promotion for this $40 monthly plan, representing a huge discount over the $75 normal price.

Given all the trickery and incompetence, I checked my account page hours later, and the price ballooned to $70.

What the heck?

Another phone call. I demanded to speak to a supervisor. A woman assured me she will work with a supervisor to resolve my problem. After explaining the situation yet again, she got back on and told me that my plan will be back on my originally grandfathered-in $50 monthly charge. Minutes later, with more deceit (you all are scum!), it’s clear that she attempted to appease me with a one-time free-month charge and then reversed course on her statement from minutes earlier that I would be back on my original grandfathered-in $50 monthly charge. Liar! So disgusting! I screamed and demand to speak to a supervisor.

My call got transferred to Ron’s voicemail. I left an irate message. She called me back, and Ron claimed to have put me back on the $50 monthly plan, locking my account at this rate.

How is your company in business with internet? The speeds suck and are inconsistent, you bounce people around to different IP addresses, and customer service is trained to willfully engage in consumer fraud.

After receiving a call from the CEO's office, the representative Samantha failed every which way, just like the trail of staff before her. She tried to insinuate that my description of the events I documented by was inaccurate. We talked about only one of these (the first phone call, in which the representative looked up my new address and confirmed that I could continue to use my modem at this location—even though this was the incorrect decision to make), but I could sense that I had to end this line of speculation so as to protect my time. I also found it amazing that a few T-Mobile reps could not tell me that the internet plan I had at my current address was in an area not served despite my claims of very slow speeds.

A short while later, Samantha instructed me to return the T-Mobile modem at a store. I had to deal with the hefty Will again. This is the idiot who opened a second line of service even though I was in the process of swapping (not adding) modems. I had some colorful language for this bozo and the uppity female rep who couldn't find my account. I stormed out, and Will, 6'7" and 300 pounds, chased after me, wanting to fight. This moron recorded me and stood in the path of my car, making me unable to exit the parking stall.

More emails to Samantha. By now, there are more than 30 points of back-and-forth correspondence. A shipping label is issued, and T-Mobile is in my rearview mirror.

I switched carriers and did a trade-in to get a new iPhone. Was told I could pay off the phone early to unlock it and use a second sim (a foreign one I need for work) while keeping the promo. Many frustrating hours later, turns out they’ve changed that policy and now you forfeit the promotion if you pay the phone off early. Have to wait TWO YEARS to unlock, and if you upgrade at that point, you’ll be locked out again. Didn’t think I could dislike anyone as much as I dislike Verizon but t-mobile has proved me wrong

My experience was great at the stand alone T-Mobile store in Bay Plaza next to Red Lobster. I was greeted as soon as I came in & waited 2 minutes before Richard came over introduced himself & asked how he could help me.  This was my 2nd trip to T-Mobile b/c at the 125th store Abraham didn't finish setting up my new phone & transferring my phone #, my SIM card to my new phone. He didn't tell me this however & I was stuck carrying my old phone & the new phone for a week until I could get back to the T-Mobile nearest me in Bay Plaza. Thankfully, I had Richard to help & finish the process, so I could have one phone instead of two!  Thank you again Richard, you were exemplary!👏🏽👏🏽👏🏽🙏🏼👍🏽

T-Mobile customer service will say ANYTHING to get you off the phone, including straight up lie about discounts or deals you're going to get. Then, when I asked about it later, the next person would be like "I don't see any notes about that, but let me do that..." Guess what? ~10 calls later - still not one person put it in the notes. Luckily still in the first 14 days, so I cancelled everything. Just lies.

There is only minimum 1 star so I have to choose that otherwise I will give zero star.  Keep on calling them in the last 6 months for my trading device issue and they drag for 6 months and today told me they can't do anything for that promotion, and I have to bear total $3000 in course of 24 months with 4 lines.

T-Mobile is untrustworthy and will not honor their agreements. Watch out for their marketing that advises you to switch to their mobile services from your current provider in order to receive a better price. In 2019, they fraudulently offered military personnel, veterans, and their families a lower price plan that would remain in effect for the lifetime of the member. As of March 13, 2025 they have indicated they will raise their prices on this plan. After contacting T-Mobile, they said this was a corporate decision and there’s nothing that anybody below the corporate structure can do to adjust prices. They also no longer have any documentation available on their website prior to 2020. I believe they did this to gain market share during the infancy of their business and now that they have a large customer base will continue to raise prices on other plans.  They will also attempt to portray that they never had a contract or made this promise. I would never have switched to another provider for a lower price if I ever thought they were going to increase their prices. The process involved in changing provider takes a lot of time and not something one takes lightly. I made this very clear with the sales associate, and she with me that this plan price was for the lifetime of the member. Shame on T-Mobile.

I want to share my extraordinarily frustrating experience with this company regarding my attempt to purchase phones under my business name. I submitted all the required paperwork to my sales agent as of March 12, and while I initially received good customer service, things quickly took a turn for the worse.

When I arrived at the store to pick up my phones and was ready to finalize the payment, I received an unexpected message from Customer Protection Review. I was directed to speak with a representative named “Sal.” Not only did he ask for the same documents I had already sent in multiple times, but he also refused to reach out to other departments to confirm their receipt. To make matters worse, his attitude towards me was unprofessional and dismissive.

Out of frustration, I escalated the situation by speaking with a supervisor named “Sree,” but even that didn’t help. After providing the necessary documents three times and spending nearly two hours in the store, I ultimately had to cancel all my lines due to the incompetence and lack of support I encountered.

If you’re a small business considering working with this company, be prepared for your time to be wasted and for unfavorable treatment. Their lack of organization and poor customer service is unacceptable. I sincerely hope they can address these issues, but my experience has left me thoroughly disappointed.

They stole my money in their bank, no explanation whasoever. When you try to call their number and put in the required into they just reroute to the beginning. They stole $21.29, glad I didn't put more into the account.

I have two phones with them-I may leave them because of this.

The service that T-Mobile‘s offers is incredible. They truly keep you connected globally however with that said it’s the people that really irritate me the customer care advocate the tech-support. They’re all rude not nice and just keep trying to charge you fake fees and manipulate your bill not a nice company But kick ass service. If you’re on a family plan, they leave you with no options or resources other rhan fees fees fees 

But shout out to Kevin. He really helped me when I was displaced and my T-Mobile service wasn’t working.

Stealing money. Locked us out of our account and tells to look at our bills... that are on the account. The customer service rep, Patrick, lied many times. I am so glad we left this company. The problem is they keep charging us for service we're not getting and CANCELLED.

Horrifying experience.  Be very careful especially with your parents and grand parents.  This bohemath of a company will absolutely take advantage of you and your elders.  They do not disclose information properly and will be dishonest about fees and charges.  I have personally never felt so ripped off.

They would not even get one star but unfortunately you have to at least give one star to leave a review. T- mobile sucks everything about them from cost to service and the by all means to get some help thru customer service there is no reason one phone an internet that’s 20 a month and 2 watches should be almost 400 a month because if you have trouble paying a bill they won’t let you set up a payment arrangement until they suspend your account then that is a 25 dollar restore fee per line on top of your bill. This company is ripping people off and everyone e is just letting it happen. Let me tell you something people there are cheaper phone services with better customer Service and plans and they don’t have you wait to be canceled before you can set up a payment arrangement and all of the small phone places there network they use is either att T-Mobile one of the big company anyway. I will never use t mobile again they suck in every way possible I wish more people would have the balls to go elsewhere and when they lose customer because of it they would maybe make some changes but because people are just staying and dealing with it they continue to screw people it’s a shame. I will be going else where.
They promise things for free but then charge you for them. Phone prices are way too high. Service is slow constantly and for two lines is astronomically high priced

T Mobile has a "billing cycle" which is the previous 30 days from the date of the bill. On that bill will be any changes made during that 30 days, plus a charge for service for the NEXT 30 days. Not only is it difficult for consumers to read the bill, their own customer service is incapable of doing so. I confirmed this when I first started with them, when I didn't understand why I was paying almost two whole months on my FIRST bill. I confirmed it again when I closed my account on February 5. I had paid through January 29th in December and the person who helped me with the transfer pin for the last of our lines confirmed that I should only owe the 6-7 days since then. My access to my account and copies of my bills was immediately taken away and reaching someone without an active T Mobile number is a nightmare. So I was mindful of needing to confirm and pay my final balance, but had a great deal of difficulty doing so. On 2/19, I was charged the full amount of the bill for 1/30 - 2/28 or $142.49. My first call took my more than 30 minutes to reach a person, because I had to finagle the system without an active number. That person insisted that my pin didn't work and that I would have to go into a store with an ID. I have used the pin many times recently and knew it was correct. Eventually they pretended not to hear me so they could hang up. I spent another 10-15 minutes reaching a person again. Magically my pin worked this time! However, this person insisted that I been charged the bill through 1/29 because that was the "billing cycle" No matter what I said, no matter what I asked them to read from the bill, they would not budge. So I asked for the supervisor. This was even worse. They refused to send me a copy of the bill and continued to insist that since the billing cycle was 12/29 - 1/29, that those were the dates of service I paid for. I have tried to find other avenues of getting this corrected, meanwhile they have stolen this money from me.
Terrible customer service and these people lying too much don’t play with them you guys are wasting time and wasting money and lots of stress come from them

I’ve been with T-Mobile over 14 years over the last four years they have gone to trash. Customer service is not helpful whatsoever. The wait phone or online  is notoriously long. You can’t get help from them over billing you even though you have a contract that says exactly how much they’re supposed to Bill. I’ve had him three times to make corrections on a bill that’s in writing a contract Horrible. I don’t have a positive thing to say about them anymore.

edits.text: I have been with T-Mobile for years, Wow, every year the customer service has got worse. Time for me to switch. text: I just contacted the better business bureau office. I'm writing in all caps to get everyone's attention who reads this comment. I bought a iPhone 12 mini . I payed off the iPhone mini 12 . I contacted customer service to make sure I was was doing everything right to unlock my phone . customer service told me  I payed off everything . I then switch carriers and found out two years later that my phone was not unlocked. I would I remember that. customer service told me to walk to a store to verify my identity . I did that when she made me make a pin I glanced at the screen and it said 70$ for 2025 owed as if my account was still open. I called customer service and they told me I owed money and they could not tell me why and that they will put a ticket in to unlock my phone. something is fishy going on . it seems like t mobile does not want its customers to move on to different service. I payed off the phone and closed the account . I will Be writing more people about t  mobile . I don't like this one bit.

Why can’t I get to my Team of Experts out of Colorado anymore? Lately every customer service rep I speak with is overseas and I have great difficulty understanding them. Today marks the day that I’ve reached my limit of trying to understand what the customer service rep is saying because their accent is thick beyond discernment. Appreciate your time!

We will not be renewing our contract

would give these guys -☆ if alowed to do so. To any one who might be thinking of using this company i was a Sprint customer for yrs when I lived in Wa state in 2016 I moved to Hi after I asked Sprint if their service worked in Hi I was told there would be no problem so I kept the service but when I got here it was not that way I couldn't make or receive calls unless i was in the middle of downtown kona anywhere else it was useless & struggled with this for a yr or so & finally they agreed to end my service with no penalty now 7yrs later they hit my credit report from a company called JEFFERSON CAPITAL SYSTEM who is charging me a bunch of money including added fees from them I don't know if something goes happened when t mobile merged with Sprint and they decided to go after a customer for their crappy service and not honor what Sprint did or what but it is a negative impact on my other wise really good credit score my stubbornness won't let me just pay it long story short don't trust this company to do what's right apparently they will go after there customers even after you are no longer with them I will continue to put this on all of their reviews to warn people about their shady practices 
THANK GOD I SWITCHED TO VERIZON I  have never had issues and have been with them for 7 yrs now

After being a customer for over a decade, the customer service and overall brant competitiveness declined so badly I finally switched out to a new, much less expensive, carrier. 

T-Mobile sent me an un-itemized final bill that was around $250 more than expected, and when I contacted them about, it included a charge for "un-returned equipment" that was returned over a decade ago when it became technologically obsolete, as well as an extra month of service (two rather than one), including a month with zero days they could have provided service to that number, as another carrier was supplying it for all the dates. 

Now, however, beause I never set up a pin while I was a customer (they allege I did, and as it's a 6-14 digit code, it's effectively un-gessable, even if I DID set it up), and because they deactivate your web account as soon as your number moves, I can't actually get anyone to speak with me on the phone numbers provided or online to get the bad charges reviewed and argued about. I also can't pay via the guest service because the numbers they provide don't link to my account, because for some reason, despite changing my name legally several years ago, and sending my notarized change of name documents, which was my current, correct name on all documents I got before I cancelled, my name was somehow reverted to my old name, which no longer corresponds to my social security number. 

I want to fairly pay them for my final month of service, but my credit score is being held hostage for $250 in goods and services I never got, in a quagmire of customer disservice. Either I pay the full amount (of which I actually owe less than half) or I am being threatened with getting reported to credit agencies.

I have twice tried physical stores, but in my state, they are all franchises and can't help with this issue, and in other states, they won't help because my account is out-of-state. I have driven over 8 hours trying to fix this at this point. 

As a former long time customer, I know current T-Mobile leadership should be flat-out embarassed of how the company went from super reliable to basic con men in less than a decade.

i tried them and left them.  slow and barely competent customer service, every issues was hours on the phone with them and pricing, they say they're cheap but thats not what i found, they steer you into more expensive plans, dont offer the cheap ones unless you ask specifically.

The worst customer service I had to deal with! Several times tried to connect with them - no answer, when they'vw finally answered, had to wait for 20 minutes to "see if my issue could be resolved", after that they told me the will do everything they can to help me, bye-bye. WTh?? I called after that several times again and again and nothing! Worthless, do not use, save your mind!

T-mobile does not work with the military.  I was mobilized and canceled and they kept charging me.  They refused to work with my parents to get this resolved since I am out of the country.  They are awful.  They take advantage of the military service personnel

Terrible Customer Service – Still No Resolution After 12 Days!

T-Mobile has completely failed me as a customer. Due to an error on their end, my phone has been locked for 12 days, and they have done nothing to fix it. Another customer mistakenly reported their phone lost or stolen and provided my IMEI number. As a result, my phone was immediately locked.

I went to my local T-Mobile store, and while the staff apologized, they told me I’d have to wait until Monday for a call to unlock my phone. That call never came. When I followed up, they pushed the issue to the fraud department and told me to wait until Wednesday. They even had the audacity to suggest I buy a $100 prepaid phone in the meantime! Friday came—still no call, still no resolution.

Frustrated, I returned to the store and was left with no choice but to return the phone and request a new one. The rep submitted a warranty request, promising a replacement by Monday. Now it's Tuesday, and I’ve heard nothing. Meanwhile, I rely on my phone for work, and this ongoing issue has caused serious disruptions to my business.

T-Mobile’s lack of urgency, accountability, and communication is beyond unacceptable. No company should be allowed to treat its customers this way. If they had to shut down for 10 days, they’d face huge financial losses—yet they expect customers to endure the same without any compensation or concern. Avoid this company if you value reliable service and customer support!

I've recently visited this T Mobile location, in Vero Beach, Florida and was thoroughly impressed with the detailed customer service provided by Ms. Carissa and Mr. Wendel.
My particular need involved the activation of a new device and the transfer of data from my old device to the new one. These two knowledgeable professionals made the process painless.
I highly recommend their location, (store # 882E located at 524 21st St.in Vero Beach.)

I was a customer with Sprint for 15 years when T-Mobile  purchased them.    I am so disappointed in the customer service I received since TMobile took over.  Yet, I stayed with them.

I recently got a new iPhone and just wanted them to transfer the data to the new phone.   I came into the local office and was told I had to be there before 7.  It was 7:02.  Besides " we have other customers".  Okay, not thrilled but I came back tonight at 6:50 pm and was turned away again -- this time being told they needed two hours.  Seems to be scrolling number.  My guess it changes if your transaction creates commission.

Time to shop for a new cell service provider.  Clearly being a long time loyal customer - 20 years between Sprint  and T-Mobile - gets you nothing at TMobile.

I’ve had AT&T, Verizon and now T-Mobile. The other services I had for over 25 years and I thought I would try TMobile. TMobile is a nightmare, worst service, worst customer service and no coverage in any areas. I’ll be switching back to Verizon but people need to know what a horrible service TMobile is.

Do NOT use T-Mobile!! They promise you certain deals then when your bill is 3 times higher than they said it would be, they say they will fix it and get back to you. They do NOT get back to you. Been going through this EVERY week for 2 months. Pathetic!

If I could give them zero stars i would.  

They are either inept or they are liars, but no matter what, don't trust anything the sales person says or the customer service. 

The sales lied, and each time I called the customer service, they contradicted each other and told me a completely different thing.  

They upsold me during the sales process and after I paid, but lied, wouldn't give me a refund.  Sad this was once a great company.  I even called customer service via their 611 number ... each time I called, I got a total different answer ... each time I asked the same question ... what are my options ... each time they definitively told me that there was no such plan that I purchased and that there was no refund and only 1 option I can do  (the funning thing is that each time I called, that option changed, and of course each option was a scam to charge more).

Instead of giving you the details, in short, while this company was once good ... they are now a bunch of either untrained (ie. they make stuff up and contradict each other)  or they are liars.  if you give them money, even IF they say it is refundable, don't believe them ... you will never get your money back.

I'm going to short TMUS - what a bad company

Issues with customer service over trade in special and receipt of my returned phone. Almost three months of calling customer service, 6 times, and being told problem has been resolved, it was not. Today, they revoked my special and required immediate back payment. Bait and switch tactics. Stay away from the new T-Mobile. Unqualified customer service. 21 year customer, can't wait for my contracts for other phones to end so I can switch carrier.

the worst customer service in the USA, a nightmare experience due to incompetence, switching from prepaid to contract, but due to incompetence they blocked my telephone number after having worked for months to distribute my business cards and look for new customers, a disaster, I do not recommend using this company

T-Mobile was my phone company for many years but the bill kept raising till it was $200, I decided to change company and they still billed me a last bill which I didn’t use the service and said they would send it to collection if I didn’t pay it, I paid a service a didn’t use and I still got another bill today stating a passed due amount of 7.41, I’m really disappointed of how they take it when you decide to move on. I feel like I’ve been robed sadly.

Absolute worst customer service I have ever experienced. I have been on the phone with them for over 2 weeks trying to activate my Hulu and not a single person they have hired can figure it out. They have wasted approximately 20 hours of my time. The. They blame it on Hulu, you’re the ones offering me the service not Hulu. Losers. Been a loyal customer for 18 years and I CANNOT wait to go cancel my plan this week and go to another company. Good riddance t-mobile.

I can't believe the ratings are so low for T-Mobile. I just had the 2nd most awesome experience with Helena N. on the chat of T-Mobile website. I had a previous awesome experience when had a cracked screen and got a free replacement, but the store wouldn't honor my warranty, so got it sent to me via UPS (it's a much longer story, but I want to focus on this current one). So tonight I noticed that my case for Revvl V+ 5G phone didn't seem to be on there correctly and noticed on the side that the back part of the phone cover itself seemed to be pulling away from the phone. I took the phone out of the case and noticed it was bulging and it was very odd. I taped it around, but decided that would search online to see what I can find about this and found that it could be caused by overcharging the phone and that sounds exactly like me, as I do that. 
I decided to go on the website and chat with someone from T-Mobile and the agent Helena N, was as nice as can be. She told me, yes that the bulge on the back has to do with the battery and that best to drain the battery to 20-30% for the health of it and will last longer. I could trade in for a free phone and also could have the activation fee waived if order myself on the app as there was a promotion going on. I loaded the app, but it was not working properly. Then she apologized and said could order it on the website as well, but I was confused as thought could only do on the app and she clarified that I need to order it myself. I finally got on the website and went in to do a trade in, but then she apologized again and said the promotion ended a few days ago, but she could order the new phone for me and would put a credit on the account for the amount of the activation. So in the end it all worked out and was able to upgrade to a new phone and have it shipped to me. I was chatting with her from 1:03 am till 2:41 am to get this done. I am so thankful to have such a great experience, as I always do! 
So as I am writing this review, I was scrolling through the chat to see how long I was on with Helena and I noticed that at one point she was confirming the address, but added an extra digit to the zipcode and I didn't even notice that until after the chat was ended, so I went back on to the same thread there and chatted with another wonderful representative, Earl and he assured me that the zipcode was correct and I was able to check the order status while he was on the chat with me to verify that it was. This only took from 2:58 am to 3:08 am. I hope others will be more open to giving them another chance, as it's hard to believe that most have had a bad experience with T-Mobile.

Terrible customer service!!! Long waits extreme incompetence, and people you can’t understand on the phone.  Took hours on hold and subsequent conversation to explain the problem.  After I quit the service and PAID outright for my phone they sent me to creditors and tried to ruin my credit.  They never told me ANYTHING, I just started receiving texts from the creditors.

I just can't believewhathas happened to t mobile after the sprint merger. I was a loyal customer for 18 years. Always paid my bill on time, it was auto drafted. Around 2020 my monthly rate for a single line began to increase. By 2025 I was up from 50 dollars a month to 97.00 a month. I called begrudgingly,after 18 years, to cancel and transfer to a more affordable service. I was told when to cancel by, when my last bill would be, and how much it would be. I set my new phone up around those dates, trying to minimize overlap in providers. When the time came i called tmobile to transfer out my number. That went easily, and I began my life with my new provider. About 5 months later I got a call from a bill collector saying I owed t mobile money. I called t mobile and they refused to tell me why I owed them money without a pin but I couldn't get a pin cause I wasn't a current customer. I spent over 4 hours on the phone with them,  mostly on hold or being transfered. I drove to a tmobile store, gave them my ID, got a new pin, went and picked up lunch for me and my cousin, we ate lunch, and I went outside to smoke a cigarette during just one of the waiting to be transferred periods. Basically, turned out the original person i talked to was wrong about the dates and I owed for the month after I transferred my line out. I paid it, begrudgingly. I filed a fraud complaint about being told one thing and being charged another (the calls are recorded after all). But i assumed it was over with and they had successfully stolen 97 dollars from me. I moved on. A month and a half later I got a notification that my credit score had dropped 20 points. I called the number one my credit report and was yelled at by a nan working for their bill collector. I had paid every month on time for 18 years and he talked to me like I was a piece of dirt and owed them 40 dollars. He refused to answer any questions just wanted to belittle me. It was insane. So I called t mobile...again. they told me now I owe them 37 dollars for it being sent to collection. I paid them, again. Spent about 3 hours on the phone this time. I filed a dispute with my credit report. I'm gonna check back with the mobile in a month to see if they're planning to steal more from me. They've really become a scam company. I used to love t mobile. I feel like I've been robbed but after I gave them my wallet they cracked me over the head with a 20 point credit reduction just to make sure I remembered getting robbed. If someone at t mobile sees this, make this right. It's absolutely insane.

Used to provide great cell service. We signed up for their home WiFi. For a week it kept going out in the afternoon.

They also promised us a gift card for switching to their WiFi service which they still haven’t given us.  We have made several calls about this. Every time there is a new excuse.

Don’t believe any gift card offer they make. They will stall awarding it to you in the hope you will go away.

I find it very disturbing that my favorite phone service intends to continue business with a known fascist. Everything else about T-Mobile is wonderful. But the fact that everyone saw Elon Musk do the Nazi salute and when I called to find out if T-Mobile was going to remain associated with a known fascist they told me I was believing propaganda. You can't unsee what Elon did on inauguration day. Call it what it is Nazi fascist salute. This is disturbing and disgusting.

Do you know what really grinds my gears? When people expect me to be a magician and have a magic wand. A few weeks ago, I lost my $10 prepaid plan without having anyway of knowing it was gone forever. Apparently, I was on a grandfathered account for nearly a year without a clue. T-Mobile got rid of it back in April but did not tell ANY of their customers the news! I checked every email and text messages from them, and DID NOT receive any notification that my prepaid account had been grandfathered and that I would never be able to get it back if I needed extra data for a month. 

That has to be some of the most ridiculous, illogical, shady business practice I've ever seen. How am I magically supposed to know this without receiving an email or text??? Believe it or not, I am not Houdini and do not have a magic wand. If I'm still getting charged $10 every month, then I'm under the assumption that the plan still exists, and if it doesn't, then the company should be forced to tell you that your account had been grandfathered, instead of having their loyal customers somehow magically know.

For the extra disrespect, the Indian supervisor I waited over 15 minutes to get on the phone with literally just lied and shooed me away. He said he would send my complaint to someone in corporate, and I'd hear something in a few business days, but over two weeks have gone by and I've heard ABSOLUTELY NOTHING from these clowns. 

Enjoy your last month of business from me because I'm switching to US Mobile starting in March.

I called T Mobile to cancel my wireless internet and was instructed to return the tower to a T Mobile store. I returned the tower in June 2, 2024 at T Mobile a Store with receipt. Contract was paid til June 20, 2024. I kept receiving bills all the way to November- spoke with Martin on Oct 11, 2024 and then to Ginger on Oct 14, 2024. Both confirmed receipt and it was just a mistake and stated it will be taken care of. Now, I'm getting collection from Cobb Law and reported to credit bureau! I have to explain all over again. T Mobile is really HORRIBLE!!. WORST service!!! Do yourself a favor- Stay Away !!

They take advantage of great costumers who been with them for years they don’t keep their word always a excuse or a reason why the bill is always going higher it’s ridiculous am only doing this for good hearted people do not trust them at their word they will take advantage of you and never have any solutions for any of your problems your having with them they take no accountability what so ever worst provider ever…

I went to the location in Countryside IL to activate my new phone and trade in my old phones.  Leo - the “ manager “ was absolutely the worst.  He gave me the worst customer service I have ever received from anyone, anywhere, ever. 
My experience with him is too involved to type here. I would like for someone to call me please.
He absolutely should not be dealing with customers in any capacity whatsoever.

I switched and guess what, after the first month, I was paying the same I paid with Verizon. A total bate and switch. Month one is low, then they tag you with all the extra charge the second month and you save nothing. And, it is a pain to change services. Don’t waste your time. Negotiate with your current provider.

After a year with T-Mobile, I am in the process of paying off my device and leaving this company for good. At first, when you’re a new customer, they lure you in with attractive promotions: discounts, waived activation fees, and supposed exclusive benefits. However, once you’re in, the manipulation, opportunism, and deception begin.

Deceptive Pricing and Dishonest Practices

One of the biggest issues with T-Mobile is its lack of pricing transparency. What you see in the app and what an agent tells you are two completely different things. There’s always an excuse:
	•	“That price was with a special discount that has now expired.”
	•	“That promotion only applies to new customers.”
	•	“Unfortunately, you no longer qualify for that rate.”

This has happened to me more than once, proving how unreliable this company is.

Terrible Coverage Despite Their Promises

I signed up for one of their most expensive plans, Go5G Plus, which supposedly offers reliable and fast coverage. The reality was entirely different:
✔ In Miami, I barely had one bar of signal.
✔ In Fort Lauderdale, there was no internet or signal. I had to place my phone next to a window just to use it.
✔ In Boston, the connection was just as bad.
✔ And that’s without mentioning other cities where the experience was equally frustrating.

T-Mobile markets itself as the most reliable network in the U.S., but that is a lie. After a year with them, I can confidently say their coverage is one of the worst.

Customer Service: Getting Worse

At least their customer service used to be fast, but now you can spend over 45 minutes waiting on a call. The little good they had, they’ve lost.

Service Quality Manipulation

When I decided to downgrade my plan because the coverage was terrible and not worth the price, instead of improving the service, they increased my home internet price from $35 to $55.

It’s worth noting that this internet doesn’t even reach 100 Mbps. If you’re streaming on a TV, you can barely use a laptop at the same time. Compared to other providers:
✔ AT&T offers 300 Mbps for $45.
✔ Xfinity offers 200 Mbps for $35 and 350 Mbps for $50.

T-Mobile, with its poor service, charges more for less. And when I attempted to cancel all my lines, they suddenly reduced my internet speed to 35 Mbps, proving that they manipulate service quality to squeeze more money from customers.

Trying to Get Home Internet: Another Scam

I tried to sign up for home internet at my friend’s place, and the app showed $50 with autopay. But when I spoke to an agent, they said “that offer was no longer available” and that the price was now $70.

Once T-Mobile has you as a customer, they raise prices and take advantage of you. They focus on luring in new customers with false promises while exploiting the ones they already have.

Conclusion: I DO NOT RECOMMEND T-MOBILE
	•	Their signal is terrible.
	•	Their pricing is unreliable.
	•	Their customer service has worsened.
	•	They make big promises but deliver nothing.
	•	They deceive and manipulate their customers.

This is an honest review. I am not making anything up or exaggerating my experience. I am sharing the truth so that others know what they’re getting into if they decide to sign up with T-Mobile.

I don’t know if reading this review will change your mind, but at the very least, you will know what to expect if you fall for their false promotions. Don’t fall into the trap.

Two bad experiences with T-Mobile: 1) I ordered a prepaid service but never received the SIM card after waiting for 2 weeks; as it turned out T-Mobile internal IT system is broken for online ordering. 2) I cancelled my credit card in my account and put in a new one ahead of time during the service period but T-Mobile still uses my old credit card and, upon failed charge, it suspended all my T-Mobile services for non-payment. Why T-Mobile service system keeps making such stupid mistakes? Their internal IT capabilitities look extremely backward!

T-Mobile is a company of crooks.  DO NOT do business with.  I landed with them through their buy-out/merger with Sprint unfortunately.  I stuck with them for a couple years before just having enough of their business practices - particularly billing.  
On Sept. 6th, 2024 I finalized my bill with T-Mobile and verified with them their would be no penalties, contracts, etc. that needed to be honored becuase it was my intent to move to Verizon that day.  The T-Mobile Rep told me I was free to go and nothing was owed and sorry to see you go.  During this conversation, I told him I had two phone numbers that would be ported, and two numbers released because we were getting new numbers to match the state we are now living in, and to please go ahead and cancel us.  The rep said it would be effective as of midnight.
Verizon took care of the number transport, and issued us two new numbers.
Fast forward 4 months (mid-month January 2025) later, I receive a paper billing statement (T-Mobile was electronic statements throughout our time with them mind you) saying I owed $490.51.  I called T-Mobile and they told me I owed for two non cancelled lines the billing statement for Sept. 19th, then Oct. 10th, Nov. 10th, and Dec. 10th, plus late fees and penalties.  This is the first I had of it.  I called T-Mobile and could not get the call escalated and I was told I had to pay it.  I reviewed everything and was told cancellation is a "mutually shared obligation".  I told them I fulfilled my obligation by telling them to cancel the lines and they did not perform on their half of the "mutual obligation".  In a nutshell, T-Mobile would not reverse the charges, citing it was correct billing and they had released the numbers due to non-payment.  I said look at the usage and you will also see non-usage from the date I told you to cancel.  T-Mobile told me too bad, I owe the money and will send it to collections.

This company is a complete mess.  They used to be good but not anymore.

1) good luck retrieving voice mail.  Often I get locked out or often I am not notified of voice mail until days later.

2) buggy platform

3) Upon cancelling, I was literally harassed by the person on the phone who wouldn't accept no for an answer.  I never got a verification that it was cancelled.  I signed up with Xfinity and I could not get the pin necessary to transfer my account. The app gave me a blank screen, the site gave me an error.  I had to go through a chat and got yet another person who wanted to know why I was leaving and tried to get me to stick with TMobile even though I cancelled my account and already signed up with another carrier.  

I had to finally call and get someone to get the pin.  

They are awful.

I had a horrible experience TRYING OUT the t-mobile home internet service. I signed up for what I thought was a trial period-- they told me that my first bill for $85 would come 30 days after I tried out the service. I got the equipment, unpackaged it, and attempted to get it set up. Despite being told that it was super easy to set up, it actually wasn't. When I had difficulty setting up the equipment, I called the technical assistance number and the lady that I spoke to couldn't answer my questions and could not help me trouble shoot the difficulties that I was experiencing in my set up. I only had the equipment for TWO DAYS and NEVER USED it, because I couldn't get it set up. I returned it through the process that they told me to (same packaging as they sent it to me in) within the same week that I received the equipment. The day that they CONFIRMED THAT THEY RECEIVED MY EQUIPMENT BACK is the SAME DAY THAT THEY BILLED ME FOR A WHOLE MONTH OF SERVICE $85. THAT same day, they ALSO told me that they were processing my refund. I called to confirm that the refund is being processed and they would only refund me $69.03! Horrible customer service and they do not stand by their agreement of billing you in 30 days after you get the equipment. I was told that if I was unhappy with the service, to return it back within 15 days for a full refund. I returned it well before the 15 days and they GAUGED me $15.97 for the pleasure of interacting with them for a service that I NEVER RECEIVED. Buyer beware.

Tried their Wireless solution.  Ordered equipment, didn't work at ALL!  Returned equipment within specified days (yes, have receipt), yet was still billed.  Called customer support, they said wait for the next bill and it will be zeroed out.   Instead, I now have a collection agency wanted to collect $157.   SCAM COMPANY!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

If i could give them zero stars i would! I decided to switch T Mobile in November. 2 weeks after having their service and i mean literally two weeks. They shut my service off. Told me i needed to pay a whole bill to have service restored, please the 20 reactivation fee. Not sure how after two weeks i had a whole bill to pay for but whatever i thought i sorted it out when i called. So, then i decide id like to upgrade because my phone sucks, and camera is broken, flash light doesn’t work it’s all glitchy. I can’t upgrade because i haven’t been with them long enough they want $900 deposit. Might as well just pay out right. I decided against it and just make do, about a week ago i check to make sure i have the protection plan, i do but its for a phone number that has no phone linked to it. They change it after i yelled and screamed and can’t use it until 2.22. I call today again about this issue, i can’t upgrade because the turn off went against me (not my fault) and i have only made like 2 payments since being with them, clearly not enough. So here i am a jacked up phone and no help from T mobile. I went to AT&T. Reduced my bill by $70, got a brand new phone and had zero issue, STAY AWAY FROM T MOBILE!

I don’t understand why so many T-Mobile customer service agents have difficulty communicating clearly or understanding basic issues. They often ask irrelevant or confusing questions, which only makes the process more frustrating for customers. Additionally, it would be great if T-Mobile implemented better voice isolation during calls so that background noise doesn’t interfere with the conversation.

This us the 2nd+ honest review  I had to write about  these scammers from April 21st 2024 to September 27th 2024 they stole $4,000 from me then from January 26th 2025 to January 31st 2025 they stole $655.74 from me after stalking me to find out if I have  a bank or cash mobey service  they can steal money from because  I refuse to set up autopay. The ceo and the executive response team  for tmobile  refuse to do anything  as they support  tmobile  and not the customer  or a customer  that is a disabled veteran.

text:
Honesty and truth. In recent Years the culture of T mobiles customer service teams.

T-Mobile's internet is unbearably slow, making basic tasks a frustrating ordeal. Despite multiple complaints, customer support has done nothing to fix the issue. I give T-Mobile one star and cannot recommend their service to anyone.

What a waste of of time i hope they fire that bum.

text: For being a loyal customer for over 8 years this type of service is unacceptable. I am extremely dissatisfied with the services provided by T-Mobile. In December 2024, I called to request the unlocking of phone for use in a different country. Despite being assured that would be unlocked within 48 to 72 hours, this turned out to be a blatant lie. Throughout January 2025, I made multiple attempts to follow up, only to be given empty promises of escalated matters and call backs that never materialized. It is now February 1st, 2025, and after being on the phone for over 1 hour and 40 minutes, T-Mobile still failed to provide a resolution. This level of incompetence and disregard for customer satisfaction is absolutely unacceptable.

Billing has always been a problem with T-Mobile. I set up automatic billing, but that ended up causing issues. They made mistakes, and it took multiple phone calls and a lot of time to get everything sorted out. Frustrated with the hassle, I decided to switch to MINT. They use the same towers but have no billing problems.

I recieved a bill for an internet devise that I had returned 2 months ago.
Spent over 30 minutes and 2 calls to "customer service" (That was a joke)
They did nothing. 

DO NOT DO BUSINESS WITH THESE THEIVES.
2many problems to list. If T - Moron's were the last cell phone company on earth. I'd still use smoke signals instead.

I bought their prepaid plan which was suppose to by $35 per month the first month it cost me $80 dollars, but I was told that the next month it would be $35. I went in yesterday to pay for the month and they charged me $65 and again was told that next month it would be $35. I would have walked out but I need my phone and knew I wouldn't have time to shop around. Next month I will be using a different service.

edits.text: They stated that their speed was good for gaming. But it's only good for games that are on your computer. I was lucky to be able to open my emails. I tried the router in different areas of the house to no avail. They said it would be $55 a month with the hotspot box and autopay, and there are no hidden fees yet my bill was $66.25.

text: Me and my wife went in to change our phones at T-Mobile because I am over 55 there was a deal so we could get over 55 plan. We contacted T-Mobile costumer service and they told us it is up to the manager of the store to refund you, 5 months later he or she has not contacted us at all. The only reason that got 1 star is it was the lowest I could put. I can't even begin to tell you the story of the outrageous crap this place is. Do not ever go with them. I was charged monthly without even a service being active. It is the biggest fraud that I dont even know how they are still in business.  SCAM FRAUD!@

We have been TMobile customers for 20-yrs. Wife and kids all used TMobile; I had AT&T provided by employer. When I retired, wanted to either have wife switch to AT&T or me switch to TMobile. BTW, we were both paying about $70 per month, and after talking to TMobile rep, he confirmed we could port my line to TMobile and bill would be an extra $10, or $80 a month…this included 2 new phones for “free”. We said ok. A couple hours later, we left with the new phones and 5g WiFi. WiFi was a 14-day test drive - if we like and keep, would be $50 per month. Our current provider for WiFi (Spectrum) was $110 monthly so switching to TMobile WiFi would save approx. $50 monthly. Unfortunately, TMobile WiFi was slow and glitchy so we returned - found out they bill us $250 for the WiFi, and we will get a $250 credit next billing cycle 🤷‍♂️. Then we get phone bill for $230, and found out rep changed our plan from Magenta to Prestige, added a 3d line, and unless we stay on Prestige plan, phones aren’t free. Very deceptive and outrageous - lied and nvr told us we were being switch from Magenta plan to Prestige plan, nvr told us we had keep WiFi and additional line that wasn’t connected to phone, and nvr told us the two free phones were part of the Prestige promotion. Stay away, get everything in writing, read paperwork, or just stay away…better providers available.
